note: Sprint 5.33 Trinity Materials Engine Result Surfacing v0.1: surfaces the Sprint 5.32 materials_engine ranking at three audit-friendly layers without touching compute_output_sha256, the reward model, governance, budget, payment, or any consensus behaviour. (1) Worker result gains a new top-level optional materials_engine_summary projection (schema trinity-materials-engine-summary/v0.1, new file at schemas/trinity/materials_engine_summary.schema.json) when backend is local_materials_engine_v01. Carries top_ranked_material + top_ranked_score, known_materials, unknown_materials, capped ranking (5 items), resolved_metrics, warnings, limitations. The summary lives OUTSIDE the hashed output_blob (which still covers only backend_result.output_obj) so adding/removing it does NOT change compute_output_sha256. A dedicated regression test runs two workers over the same classifier-derived request and asserts compute_output_sha256 still matches byte-for-byte — the cross-worker replay contract from Sprint 5.12 is preserved. (2) Operator run gains two new top-level optional fields: materials_engine_summary_count (int, default 0) and materials_engine_top_materials (deduplicated sorted array, default []). Always present even for runs with no materials_engine workers so downstream consumers see a uniform schema. The operator loop computes the roll-up after the worker step by walking worker_out/TRINITY_USEFUL_COMPUTE_RESULT_*.json — read-only, never modifies worker results, never invokes the network. (3) Queue dashboard gains five new optional fields per latest_items entry: materials_engine_summary_count, materials_engine_top_material, materials_engine_known_count, materials_engine_unknown_count, materials_engine_warnings_count. The dashboard reads the operator_run roll-up when present and falls back to walking worker_out directly otherwise. HTML render gains a new materials_engine column between watchdog and operator_run showing 'PrOx (known 2, unknown 0)' painted purple #a78bfa via the existing _e() html.escape helper — Sprint 5.28 privacy contracts (no JS, no external assets, no absolute paths) preserved end-to-end. Schemas extended additively: useful_compute_result (+materials_engine_summary optional + scientific_intake added to task_type enum to patch a latent pre-Sprint-5.20 schema gap), useful_compute_operator_run (+materials_engine_summary_count + materials_engine_top_materials optional), task_queue_dashboard (+5 materials_engine_* fields on latest_items optional). All schema $id values unchanged. Pre-Sprint-5.33 artifacts on disk still validate against the extended schemas. Sprint 5.32's manual_review_required=true contract for real_backend is preserved and re-asserted by a regression test — the surfacing layer is metadata-for-humans, not a policy change. Non-goals for v0.1 explicitly documented: no change to compute_output_sha256 formula, no change to reward / governance / budget / payment behaviour, no flipping manual_review_required, no new RPC / endpoint / file format, no bulky data (no property_table, no per-metric breakdown in the summary), no new dashboard JS. Tests: 1300+ passing on the deployment host (1288 prior + 13 new). Manual demo over the Sprint 5.31 classifier request: worker result carries top_ranked_material=PrOx with score 0.822; both workers' compute_output_sha256 identical (51dec30674105a4b...); operator_run.materials_engine_summary_count=2 with top_materials=[PrOx]; dashboard JSON+HTML shows 'PrOx (known 2, unknown 0)' in the new materials_engine column. Pure scripts+schemas+docs+tests merge: zero src/, zero consensus, zero wallet/payment/broadcast changes.
